title: Number of people living in extreme poverty
subtitle: Extreme poverty is defined as living below the [International Poverty Line](#dod:international-poverty-line) of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Number of people living in extreme poverty

Extreme poverty is defined as living below the International Poverty Line of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This stacked area chart illustrates the global population distribution between those living in extreme poverty and those not, spanning the years 1990 to 2026. The vertical axis measures the number of people in billions, ranging from 0 to 9 billion. The lower section, shaded in dark pink, represents the population living in extreme poverty. In 1990, this group stood at approximately 2.3 billion people. Over the subsequent decades, this area shows a consistent downward trend, dropping below 2 billion around 2005 and falling below 1 billion by 2015. By the end of the timeline in 2026, the dark pink area narrows significantly, indicating a value well below 1 billion. Conversely, the upper section, shaded in light orange, represents the population not in extreme poverty. This area demonstrates substantial growth, expanding from roughly 3 billion in 1990 to over 8 billion in 2026. The total height of the combined areas rises steadily from just over 5 billion in 1990 to approximately 8.3 billion in 2026, reflecting overall global population growth. The data presented is expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices and relates to income or consumption per capita depending on the specific country and year. The visual trajectory highlights a shift where the majority of the global population moves out of the extreme poverty category while the total number of people increases.
